evals.name: client-cancellation
evals.prompt: I'm adding stop/cancel support to my ACP client (an editor extension written in TypeScript). When the user hits the stop button mid-turn, what exactly does the protocol require me to do, and what should I expect back from the agent? I want to make sure I don't show a scary error when the user cancels.
evals.expected_output: Accurate v1 cancellation flow: send the session/cancel notification, preemptively mark unfinished tool calls cancelled, respond to pending session/request_permission with the cancelled outcome, expect the session/prompt response with stopReason cancelled (not an error), and keep accepting session/update notifications until that response arrives.
assertions: Says to send the session/cancel notification (a notification, not a request expecting a response)
Says the client MUST respond to any pending session/request_permission requests with the 'cancelled' outcome
Says the client SHOULD preemptively mark unfinished tool calls from the current turn as cancelled
Says the agent responds to the original session/prompt with stopReason 'cancelled' — a normal response, not a JSON-RPC error — which is exactly why no error should be shown to the user
Mentions the client should still accept session/update notifications that arrive after sending session/cancel

evals.name: v1-to-v2-migration
evals.prompt: our acp agent currently targets protocol v1 and uses fs/read_text_file, fs/write_text_file and the terminal/* methods heavily. what would it take to also support v2? list the breaking changes that hit us specifically and how we're supposed to replace the removed APIs
evals.expected_output: Correct v2 migration guidance: fs/* and terminal/* are removed in v2 in favor of client-provided MCP servers; prompt lifecycle redesign (session/prompt response acknowledges acceptance, stop reason arrives via state_update); authenticate/logout renamed to auth/login and auth/logout; session/load replaced by session/resume with replayFrom; support both versions side by side via per-connection version negotiation.
assertions: States that fs/read_text_file, fs/write_text_file, and all terminal/* methods are removed in v2
States the replacement is client-provided MCP servers (the client exposes file/terminal functionality as MCP tools)
Mentions the prompt lifecycle redesign: the session/prompt response only acknowledges acceptance, and progress/completion (including the stop reason) arrive via state_update session updates
Mentions authenticate → auth/login and/or logout → auth/logout renames, or session/load being replaced by session/resume with replayFrom
Recommends supporting v1 and v2 side by side via per-connection version negotiation (not dropping v1), and/or notes v2 is still labeled draft

evals.name: mcp-passthrough
evals.prompt: How does an ACP editor hand its user-configured MCP servers over to the agent? Which MCP transports can I assume the agent supports, and how do I check before sending an HTTP server config? Show the JSON for a session/new request with one stdio server and one HTTP server.
evals.expected_output: Explains that MCP server configs are passed in the mcpServers array of session/new (and load/resume), stdio is the mandatory baseline transport, HTTP/SSE require checking agentCapabilities.mcpCapabilities in the initialize response, and shows a correct session/new JSON with cwd plus stdio (name/command/args/env) and HTTP (type, name, url, headers) entries.
assertions: Explains MCP server configs are passed in the mcpServers array of session/new (and session/load / session/resume)
States stdio is the transport every agent MUST support (the safe baseline assumption)
States HTTP support must be checked via mcpCapabilities.http in the agentCapabilities of the initialize response before sending an HTTP config
Shows a session/new JSON with an absolute cwd plus a stdio server entry using name/command/args (env as name/value pairs)
The HTTP server entry in the JSON uses type: "http" with name, url, and headers fields
